confirmed.note: MustHold vs MustNotHold propagators.install lambdas are near-identical: same capture skeleton + same run_lex_pass(...) call, differing only by gt<->lt flag names and swapped vars_1/vars_2 (negation). AI 'one copied block per reif enum branch' pattern.

Inside the Undecided branch the same MustHold/MustNotHold run_lex_pass blocks are re-pasted a second time (gt-flags vs lt-flags, vars swapped), duplicating the pair already present in the top-level overloaded visitor.

false_positives.what_happened: New constraint file repeats the per-constraint class boilerplate shared by ~71 files: identical include preamble, identical 'using std::...' block, the '#if defined(__cpp_lib_print)' print-vs-fmt idiom, and the fixed clone()/install()/prepare()/define_proof_model()/install_propagators()/s_exprify() NVI skeleton. Token/line detector sees a high-weight cross-file match on the scaffolding even though every propagator body is unique.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: strip a per-language 'file preamble' window before hashing — drop the leading contiguous run of #include / using-declaration / preprocessor (#if/#else/#endif) lines from the token stream, and ignore matches whose token span is dominated by declarator/signature tokens with empty bodies. Add a min-distinct-identifier threshold so a block whose only varying tokens are the class name is not counted.

false_positives.what_happened: Commit is a directory move (gcs/constraints/{ => table}/table.cc rename, -R in git) plus extraction of negative_table. The shared include/using preamble and the 'depointinate' SFINAE helper (present only in table.cc + negative_table.cc) are genuine carried-over boilerplate from the same family, flagged as cross-file dup. It is a refactor split, not new copied logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: honour git rename/copy detection (already added for #061/#064) and also suppress matches between a moved file and its rename source within the same commit; treat extract-into-sibling (R/C similarity >50% on the source) as a move, not a clone.

false_positives.what_happened: removed-boost commit rewrites every example/benchmark main() to the same cxxopts CLI preamble ('cxxopts::ParseResult options_vars; options_vars = options.parse(argc, argv);' plus the standard --help/--prove option set). 36 .cc files share this verbatim argument-parsing block — a project-wide example template, not copy-pasted business logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add an allowlist / down-weight for known CLI-bootstrap idioms (cxxopts/CLI11/argparse parse blocks) and for code under examples/ + */benchmarks/ dirs; or require that a cross-file match contain at least one project-domain identifier (constraint/propagator/proof symbol) before reporting.

false_positives.what_happened: linear_{greater,less}_than_equal.cc are thin subclass families: each ctor just forwards to ReifiedLinearInequality (greater = negate(coeff)+(-value) of less). The files mirror each other almost line-for-line because they are intentional symmetric delegating wrappers with no real body.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: ignore cross-file matches whose matched span is entirely constructor-delegation / base-initializer-list with empty bodies (no statements in braces); a sibling-class-family of pure forwarding ctors is structural symmetry, not duplication worth reporting.

cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph: nodes=284 edges=1312 sccs_total=284 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1. Author code (gcs/) is acyclic; no vendored library cycle either.
